_comment: Codex CLI hook wiring — mirrors .claude/settings.json and .github/hooks/hooks.json. Same four guard scripts; Codex matchers use its tool names (apply_patch|Edit|Write for file writes, Bash for shell). Codex delivers the same stdin JSON (tool_name/tool_input/tool_response) and blocks on exit code 2, so the existing scripts apply. NOTE: Codex routes file edits through apply_patch (a patch diff), so validate-immutable-paths must read the patch target, not only tool_input.file_path — verify per .codex/VERIFICATION.md. Windows uses the .ps1 variants below; on macOS/Linux point command at the matching .github/hooks/scripts/*.sh.

hooks.statusMessage: Blocking writes to immutable paths + git push/merge (human-only)
